artifact: Q-Periapt — assumption-minimal, cross-substrate binding assurance for PQ/T hybrid KEMs
purpose: Expected-result manifest so a reviewer can detect data drift: every value below is measured, not asserted. Run `sh artifact/smoke.sh` and compare; a full frozen run is in artifact/ci-snapshot.log.

provenance.note: Values captured against the tree at this commit. The artifact-only files (artifact/*, this manifest) added afterward change no tested code, so the expected counts still hold.

smoke.needs: Rust toolchain + a C compiler (no Docker / wasm-pack / Node / network beyond cargo fetch)

steps.name: rustls loopback handshake
steps.expect: 1 passed (real in-memory TLS 1.3 handshake over the hybrid group)

easycrypt.full_machinecheck: BindingViaCR.ec checks and negative-controls.sh passes 7/7 under the pinned EasyCrypt r2026.06 container (formal/Dockerfile); this is the hard CI gate (Tier 2, not part of the smoke).

footprint_bytes.note: Platform-dependent; regenerate per host with `sh paper/footprint.sh`.

ci_snapshot.note: A real local run of `artifact/smoke.sh` plus `cargo test --workspace`, clippy, and fmt. Verify with `shasum -a 256 artifact/ci-snapshot.log`. This is a local capture, NOT a GitHub Actions export.
